metadata.title: Measure the Routing Matrix: Which Tasks Belong to Claude vs. Human
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: Five tasks straddling CoWork and Claude Code — adding one signal, has side effects on production systems, reclassifies three of them.

Four routing tiers: Chat for quick one-offs, CoWork for multi-step delegated tasks, Claude Code for autonomous builds, Human-only for regulated or irreversible actions. The routing decision is not about capability — Claude can attempt most tasks. It is about accountability: who owns the outcome when something goes wrong?
Classify each of these tasks into Chat, CoWork, Claude Code, or Human-only. For each, state the key signal that determined the classification.

props.command: claude "Classify each of these tasks into: Chat, CoWork, Claude Code, or Human-only. For each, state the key signal that determined the classification." < tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: The routing matrix script classifies tasks, producing a routing tier, key signal, and confidence score for each.

20 tasks classified: 8 Chat, 7 CoWork, 3 Claude Code, 2 Human-only. Two tasks flagged as boundary cases — schedule a meeting, Chat vs CoWork; run the monthly report, CoWork vs Claude Code. The model flags these as ambiguous rather than forcing a classification.
Feed 5 tasks straddling CoWork and Claude Code boundary. Add signal: has side effects on production systems.

props.command: claude "Re-classify these 5 tasks. Additional signal for all: has side effects on production systems." < boundary_tasks.txt

beats.narration_text: With the production-side-effects signal, 3 of the 5 reclassify from Claude Code to CoWork — they now require a human checkpoint before execution. The signal did not change Claude's capability; it changed the accountability requirement.

The routing matrix gives every task a tier, a signal, and an accountability assignment. The production-systems signal is the most important one — if the AI's mistake is irreversible, the tier must require a human checkpoint.
Next: build a file-rename tool with a mandatory dry-run before anything changes.
Nik Bear Brown. Brutalist and Educational AI. nikbearbrown.com.
